indexes.name: tier_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX tier_pkey ON public.tier USING btree (id)

indexes.name: customer_new_id_key
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX customer_new_id_key ON public.customer USING btree (id)

indexes.name: customer_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX customer_pkey ON public.customer USING btree (id)

indexes.name: customer_old_id_key
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX customer_old_id_key ON public.customer USING btree (old_id)

indexes.name: customer_path_gist_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX customer_path_gist_idx ON public.customer USING gist (path)

indexes.name: customer_path_btree_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX customer_path_btree_idx ON public.customer USING btree (path)

indexes.name: cf_org_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX cf_org_pkey ON public.cf_org USING btree (id)

indexes.name: cf_org_customer_id_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX cf_org_customer_id_idx ON public.cf_org USING btree (customer_id)

tables.name: public.meter
tables.comment: A Meter reads usage information from a system in Cloud.gov. It also namespaces natural IDs for resources and resource_kinds; meter + natural_id is a primary key.

indexes.name: meter_name_key
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX meter_name_key ON public.meter USING btree (name)

tables.name: public.resource_kind
tables.comment: ResourceKind represents a particular kind of billable resource. Note that natural_id can be empty because some meters may only read one kind of resource, and that resource kind may not have a unique identifier in the target system; it is uniquely identified by the meter name only.

indexes.name: resource_kind_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX resource_kind_pkey ON public.resource_kind USING btree (meter, natural_id)

indexes.name: idx_resource_kind_meter_natural_id
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_resource_kind_meter_natural_id ON public.resource_kind USING btree (meter, natural_id)
indexes.comment: Enables efficient deduplicated inserts using BulkCreateResourceKinds function.

indexes.name: resource_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX resource_pkey ON public.resource USING btree (meter, natural_id)

indexes.name: resource_meter_natural_id_uq
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX resource_meter_natural_id_uq ON public.resource USING btree (meter, natural_id)

indexes.name: idx_resource_meter_natural_id
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_resource_meter_natural_id ON public.resource USING btree (meter, natural_id)
indexes.comment: Enables efficient deduplicated inserts using BulkCreateResources function.

indexes.name: resource_cf_org_id_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX resource_cf_org_id_idx ON public.resource USING btree (cf_org_id)

constraints.name: fk_cf_kind_id
constraints.def: FOREIGN KEY (meter, kind_natural_id) REFERENCES resource_kind(meter, natural_id)

columns.name: created_at
columns.comment: CreatedAt must be a time in UTC.

columns.name: periodic
columns.comment: Periodic is true if a reading was taken automatically as part of the periodic usage measurement schedule, or false if it was requested manually.

columns.name: created_at_utc
columns.extra_def: GENERATED ALWAYS AS (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC'::text) STORED
columns.comment: CreatedAtUTC supplements CreatedAt, which does not have a timezone. Values must be inserted into CreatedAt in UTC by the client. CreatedAt has a unique index on it to enforce readings being taken at most hourly. Because the index uses functions that are not volatility level IMMUTABLE, it cannot be used on a column with a timezone; hence the supplementary generated column.

indexes.name: reading_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX reading_pkey ON public.reading USING btree (id)

indexes.name: idx_reading_created_at
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX idx_reading_created_at ON public.reading USING btree (created_at)

indexes.name: reading_hourly_uq
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX reading_hourly_uq ON public.reading USING btree (date_trunc('hour'::text, created_at))

indexes.name: reading_created_at_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX reading_created_at_idx ON public.reading USING btree (created_at)

indexes.name: reading_created_at_utc_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX reading_created_at_utc_idx ON public.reading USING btree (created_at_utc)

columns.name: amount_microcredits
columns.comment: AmountMicrocredits is a denormalized column that is calculated from the Price of the ResourceKind that was applicable when the measurement was taken (based on the time of the Reading). The value is persisted here for simpler rollups and auditing.

columns.name: transaction_id
columns.comment: TransactionID is the transaction that accounts for this usage, typically a "post usage" transaction.

indexes.name: measurement_reading_id_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX measurement_reading_id_idx ON public.measurement USING btree (reading_id)

indexes.name: measurement_meter_resource_natural_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX measurement_meter_resource_natural_idx ON public.measurement USING btree (meter, resource_natural_id)

constraints.name: fk_resource_id
constraints.def: FOREIGN KEY (meter, resource_natural_id) REFERENCES resource(meter, natural_id)

indexes.name: account_type_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX account_type_pkey ON public.account_type USING btree (id)

indexes.name: account_type_name_uidx
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX account_type_name_uidx ON public.account_type USING btree (name)

indexes.name: account_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX account_pkey ON public.account USING btree (id)

indexes.name: account_unique
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX account_unique ON public.account USING btree (id, type)

indexes.name: account_customer_type_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX account_customer_type_idx ON public.account USING btree (customer_id, type)

columns.name: customer_id
columns.comment: CustomerID is somewhat redundant because the Entry rows associated with a Transaction are associated with Accounts, which are associated with a Customer. However, we have to create a Transaction before we create an Entry (see post_usage, ins_tx as an example). To join Measurements, Transactions, Entries, and Accounts, Transaction needs a CustomerID.

indexes.name: transaction_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX transaction_pkey ON public.transaction USING btree (id)

indexes.name: entry_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX entry_pkey ON public.entry USING btree (transaction_id, account_id)

constraints.name: transaction_balances_chk
constraints.def: CREATE CONSTRAINT TRIGGER transaction_balances_chk AFTER INSERT OR DELETE OR UPDATE ON public.entry DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_transaction_balances()

triggers.name: transaction_balances_chk
triggers.def: CREATE CONSTRAINT TRIGGER transaction_balances_chk AFTER INSERT OR DELETE OR UPDATE ON public.entry DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_transaction_balances()

indexes.name: price_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX price_pkey ON public.price USING btree (id)

constraints.name: fk_resource_kind
constraints.def: FOREIGN KEY (meter, kind_natural_id) REFERENCES resource_kind(meter, natural_id)

indexes.name: resource_node_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX resource_node_pkey ON public.resource_node USING btree (customer_id, resource_natural_id)

indexes.name: resource_path_gist_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX resource_path_gist_idx ON public.resource_node USING gist (path)

indexes.name: resource_path_btree_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX resource_path_btree_idx ON public.resource_node USING btree (path)

indexes.name: customer_path_idx
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX customer_path_idx ON public.resource_node USING btree (customer_id, path)

indexes.name: resource_node_path_uq
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX resource_node_path_uq ON public.resource_node USING btree (customer_id, path)

relations.def: FOREIGN KEY (meter, kind_natural_id) REFERENCES resource_kind(meter, natural_id)
FOREIGN KEY (meter, resource_natural_id) REFERENCES resource(meter, natural_id)
FOREIGN KEY (meter, kind_natural_id) REFERENCES resource_kind(meter, natural_id)

functions.name: public.bounds_month_prev
functions.arguments: as_of timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(), tz text DEFAULT 'America/New_York'::text

functions.name: public.update_measurement_microcredits
functions.arguments: as_of timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now()

functions.name: public.post_usage
functions.arguments: as_of timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now()

functions.name: public.lca
functions.arguments: ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree

functions.name: public.lca
functions.arguments: ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree

functions.name: public.lca
functions.arguments: ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree, ltree

driver.name: postgres
driver.database_version: PostgreSQL 15.15 (Debian 15.15-1.pgdg13+1) on aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, compiled by gcc (Debian 14.2.0-19) 14.2.0, 64-bit
